rationale: The binding prior explicitly states the app replaces a hand-maintained Excel retirement model that is 'hard to keep updated.' This directly names the core pain point as stale/manual asset values, making automated real-time asset updates the primary job-to-be-done. A retirement model by nature tracks progress toward a plan, so actual-vs-plan tracking is the natural companion purpose. Option 2 (prettier UI) is ruled out because it doesn't fix the update problem the user actually has. Option 3 (full financial planning suite) contradicts the single-household, focused scope established in the prior — it would be over-engineering a personal replacement tool.
